A windmill core principle is converting the kinetic energy of wind into mechanical energy. Windmills have blades or vanes that are designed to catch the wind. As the wind blows against the blades, it creates a force that causes them to spin or rotate around a central shaft. The rotating central shaft is connected to a mechanism that converts the rotational energy into useful work. The blades of a windmill sweep out a circle of area A and the wind (density ρ) flows at a velocity V perpendicular to the circle, then:
What is the mass of the air passing through it in time t?

output.text:
Phosphoric acid ionizes in three steps with their ionization constant values Ka1, Ka2 and Ka3, respectively, while K is the overall ionization constant. Which of the following statements are true?
A. log K = log Ka1 + log Ka2 + log Ka3
B. H3PO4 is a stronger acid than H2PO4− and HPO42−.
C. Ka1 > Ka2 > Ka3
D. Ka1 = (Ka3 + Ka2) / 2
Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
(1) A and B only
(2) A and C only
(3) B, C and D only
(4) A, B and C only

have blades or vanes that are designed to catch the wind. As the wind blow against the blades, it

connected to a mechanism that converts the rotational energy into useful work.

perpendicular to the circle, then 
What is the kinetic energy of the air passed in time t?
